seo_title: Making the Case for Curiosity-Driven Science: Why Funding Matters
meta_description: MIT President Sally Kornbluth warns of the dangers of draining the US research pipeline, highlighting the importance of basic science and its impact on…
body_html:
According to MIT President Sally Kornbluth, the US research ecosystem is facing growing challenges due to strained funding for America's top research universities.

Consequences of Draining the Research Pipeline

The US pipeline of basic science could have enormous negative ramifications for the country in the long run. Draining this pipeline could lead to a shortage of new cancer therapies and AI technologies. Uncertainty and lost funding are having a huge impact on the talent pipeline, threatening the next generation of scientific researchers.

Universities are where most of the science with a long pathway to impact starts. Basic immunotherapy research led to cancer therapies, highlighting the importance of this pipeline.

Patience is required for scientific breakthroughs, and universities play a critical role in training graduate students. The US pipeline of basic science is a critical asset for the country.

Addressing Skepticism About Higher Education "As one of the top institutions in the world, it's part of our responsibility to articulate the importance of science," said Kornbluth. "Behind the scenes, I am — along with many other university presidents — I am in D.C. all the time now. I want to speak to Congressmen and women, Senators, people in the executive branch to explain the importance of what we are doing." Relevance to Industry and Society

The pipeline of basic science is critical for innovation and economic growth. Universities play a unique role in training graduate students, who become the next generation of scientific researchers. The US research ecosystem faces significant challenges due to strained funding, highlighting the need for increased investment.

source.title: Making the case for curiosity-driven science
source.summary: President Sally Kornbluth spoke in front of a packed crowd about growing challenges to the U.S. research ecosystem as funding for America’s top research universities becomes increasingly strained.
